entry.title: The Missing Semester of Your CS Education
entry.content: This is what I hope for very long time…
entry.summary: This is what I hope for very long time…

entry.title: 6.824 Distributed Systems
entry.content: Case study is the core of this course, means many industrial level production distributed systems…
entry.summary: Case study is the core of this course, means many industrial level production distributed systems…

entry.title: Self-Check Card II, Systems Design
entry.content:
Master all the basic systems design principles, they are unescapable, and really helpful in your day to day work…

…The only thing I can do is to try to solve as many as problems, and analyze them thoroughly, case by case, and repetitively…

Let’s speed up the learning process!

No.Quiz
1Pastebin.com(or Bit.ly)
entry.summary: Master all the basic systems design principles, they are unescapable, and really helpful in your day to day work…

entry.title: Self-Check Card I, Algorithms
entry.content:
Master all the basic algorithms, they are unescapable.

Problems those I can’t come up with solutions directly… Need to rethink them.

No.Quiz
360Sort Transformed Array
490The Maze
540Single Element in a Sorted Array
555Split Concatenated Strings
604Design Compressed String Iterator
621Task Scheduler
670Maximum Swap
750Number Of Corner Rectangles
856Score of Parentheses
915Partition Array into Disjoint Intervals
1025Divisor Game
1062Longest Repeating Substring
1063Number of Valid Subarrays
1064Fixed Point
1081Smallest Subsequence of Distinct Characters
1093Statistics from a Large Sample
1106Parsing A Boolean Expression
1130Minimum Cost Tree From Leaf Values
1156Swap For Longest Repeated Character Substring
1215Stepping Numbers
1227Airplane Seat Assignment Probability
1238Circular Permutation in Binary Representation
1286Iterator for Combination

Bit Mask1239. Maximum Length of a Concatenated String with Unique Characters

Tree315. Count of Smaller Numbers After Self
Tree776. Split BST
Tree834. Sum of Distances in Tree

entry.summary: Master all the basic algorithms, they are unescapable.

entry.title: Algorithm Analysis, Learning Report
entry.content:
I need note this, by writing the mathematical formulas by hands, I can retain them.

The best way to master these materials is through analyzing the real problems, and think them repetitively.

An illustration image of asymptotic notations involved in discussion.

Here, I need review the three notations in the asymptotic analysis of algorithms,

First, - notation

Definition:

Second, - notation

Definition:

Third, - notation

Definition:

Other notations in lectures,

Fourth, - notation

Definition:

Another popular way to illustrate this,

Fifth, - notation

Definition:

Another popular way to illustrate this,

Great way to draw an analogy between the asymptotic comparsion of two functions and and comparison of two real numbers and :
entry.summary: I need note this, by writing the mathematical formulas by hands, I can retain them.

entry.title: 6.s081 Operating System Engineering
entry.content:
Being a craftsman… Only way to learn to do systems…

By following this course, I want to sum up the important concepts in operating systems field…

Theory Note, less is more!
entry.summary: Being a craftsman… Only way to learn to do systems…

entry.title: 15-213 Introduction to Computer Systems
entry.content:
Excellent opportunity to explore computer systems…

Labs are the core of this class, at the meantime, I should also catch up with readings, remember the only way to learn system is through doing system.

Chapter 1: A Tour of Computer Systems

I encounter a very good image to illustrate the general abstractions in computer systems…

Chapter 2: Representing and Manipulating Information

Some tips gained from this chapter,

  • Think about how to implement XOR: x ^ y = (x & ~y) | (~x & y), if only permit using &, ~, how about the solution?
  • 将小范围有符号数转为大范围无符号数时，C标准要求先改变范围，再改变符号！
  • How C language treat that the number convertion from small range to big range?
  • 逻辑运算关系 x ^ y = (x & ~y) | (~x & y)
  • 浮点数如何在计算机中表示？科学计算需要注意什么？
  • CPU与内存之间的各级cache，由于访问时间的限制，完全由硬件电路实现，其实就是一个硬件哈希表
No.Concept
1A block is a fixed-sized packet of information that moves back and forth between a cache and main memory (or a lower-level cache).
2A line is a container in a cache that stores a block, as well as other information such as the valid bit and the tag bits.
3A set is a collection of one or more lines. Sets in direct-mapped caches consist of a single line. Sets in set associative and fully associative caches consist of multiple lines.

Chapter 7: Linking

  • 区分weak symbol与strong symbol，这会引起很难发现的bug
No.Rule
1Multiple strong symbols are not allowed
2Given a strong symbol and multiple weak symbols, choose the strong symbol
3Given multiple weak symbols, choose any of the weak symbols
  • 链接器的主要作用：symbol resolution与relocation

Chapter 8: Exceptional Control Flow

  • 异常控制流，分类与梳理
ClassCauseAsync/SyncReturn behavior
InterruptSignal from I/O deviceAsyncAlways returns to next instruction
TrapIntentional exceptionSyncAlways returns to next instruction
FaultPotentially recoverable errorSyncMight return to current instruction
AbortNonrecoverable errorSyncNever returns
  • 区分概念：parallel flows与concurrent flows，前者是后者的子集
  • 梳理概念：进程的若干状态
StatusDescription
RunningThe process is either executing on the CPU or is waiting to be executed and will eventually be scheduled by the kernel
StoppedThe execution of the process is suspended and will not be scheduled
TerminatedThe process is stopped permanently
  • 僵尸进程：a terminated process that has not yet been reaped is called a zombie
  • 信号量理解：
    • 定义一：higher-level software form of exceptional control flow
    • 定义二：signals are the software analog of hardware interrupts
  • 思考信号量实际使用场景中的坑？
  • 继续读书shell实现，rand检测

Chapter 9: Virtual Memory

  • 研究简化的case study，最利于理解复杂的概念
  • 理解memory mapping为什么给很多任务都能提供便利
  • DRAM caches are fully associative
  • 结合mmap再次理解fork，execve等系统调用

Chapter 10: System-Level I/O

  • 理解Linux的I/O层次：内核提供什么接口？用户态利用内核接口可以额外做哪些事情？
ClassContent
High-LevelStandard I/O functions are implemented “on top of” (using) the Unix I/O functions
Low-LevelUnix I/O is implemented in the operating system kernel
  • 思考一下short counts问题为什么会发生？
  • 理解linux内核如何维护open file，这跟file share关系密切
  • 10.9 Putting It Together: Which I/O Functions Should I Use? 重读，理清概念
